summary: Matched the Dolphin DVDInit routine in C and improved DVD-related assembly labeling. The PR moved DVDInit out of asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s into new src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c, added that C object to obj_files.mk before the remaining dvd.s object, and renamed several anonymous DVD globals and labels across dvd.s, dvdfs.s, and dvdlow.s. The implementation preserves existing low-level DVD initialization behavior, including alarm setup, DVD filesystem and waiting-queue initialization, interrupt handler registration, thread queue initialization, direct DVD hardware register writes at 0xCC006000, boot magic checks, OSReport messages, and __fstLoad for JTAG boot.

key_files.role: Removed asm DVDInit, moved its string data to C ownership, and applied DVD global/label naming cleanup.
Renamed DVDFS globals and introduced __DVDThreadQueue storage in sbss.
Renamed low-level DVD globals and timeout handler references such as CommandList, Callback, NextCommandNumber, and AlarmHandlerForTimeout.
Added src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c.o to the DVD object list before asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s.o.

what_changed: Deleted the assembly definition of DVDInit from asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s and added an equivalent 47-line C implementation in src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c.
Added src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk between dvdfs.s.o and dvd.s.o, allowing only DVDInit to be compiled from C while the rest of dvd.s remains assembly.
Represented the DVD hardware register block at fixed address 0xCC006000 with a small struct named IO, then used IO.unk0 = 0x2A and IO.unk4 = 0 to match the original register stores.
Modeled bootInfo as a pointer to a small extern struct with eight u32 words followed by boot_magic, matching the asm access to bootInfo + 0x20.

Moved DVDInit string literals into C: "app booted via JTAG
", "load fst
", "app booted from bootrom
", and "bootrom
".
Renamed dvd.s sbss symbols from raw lbl_* names to more meaningful DVD globals including IDShouldBe, PausingFlag, FirstTimeInBootrom, DVDInitialized, and __DVDThreadQueue references.
Renamed dvdfs.s lbl_804D7278 to BootInfo and added __DVDThreadQueue in sbss.
Renamed dvdlow.s symbols including lbl_804A7488 to CommandList, lbl_804D7238 to Callback, lbl_804D7274 to NextCommandNumber, and references to lbl_80336E98 to AlarmHandlerForTimeout.
Removed many .global directives for internal branch-table or local labels in dvd.s, reducing accidental exported-label noise.
smart_moves: The PR decompiled a small, isolated entry point first: DVDInit depends on existing asm routines but does not require converting the whole DVD state machine at once.
Adding dvd.c.o immediately before dvd.s.o in obj_files.mk preserved link order while replacing only the matched routine.
Using extern declarations for existing assembly symbols let the new C function call and share state with the still-assembly DVD subsystem.
The bootInfo struct was sized to place boot_magic at offset 0x20, which is the access pattern shown in the removed assembly.
The fixed-address IO struct made the hardware register writes readable while retaining the exact 0xCC006000/0xCC006004 behavior.
Meaningful sbss names replaced anonymous labels before or alongside C conversion, making later DVD decompilation easier and less error-prone.
decomp_lessons: For SDK subsystem bring-up code, it can be effective to match one initializer in C while leaving callbacks and state-machine internals in asm.
When C code accesses still-assembly globals, rename the asm storage labels first so the C externs express intent and reduce future churn.
Small placeholder structs are useful for matching pointer-plus-offset loads when the complete SDK struct is not yet recovered; here boot_magic is explicitly placed at offset 0x20.
Memory-mapped I/O can be represented as a fixed-address struct when the compiler and project syntax support it; this keeps register stores readable without hiding exact addresses.
Removing unnecessary .global directives on local jump labels helps distinguish real exported symbols from internal switch/case or branch targets.
String literal relocation matters when moving assembly into C: the original .data strings for DVDInit were removed from dvd.s because the C function now emits/owns them.
naming_conventions: DVD SDK internal functions keep double-underscore prefixes, e.g. __DVDFSInit, __DVDClearWaitingQueue, __DVDInitWA, __DVDInterruptHandler, __fstLoad, and __DVDThreadQueue.

Recovered DVD global state uses SDK-like PascalCase or mixed SDK names: DVDInitialized, FirstTimeInBootrom, IDShouldBe, PausingFlag, PauseFlag, AutoFinishing, ResetRequired, Canceling, CancelCallback.
Low-level DVD globals were renamed toward descriptive nouns: CommandList, Callback, NextCommandNumber.
Callback/state function names in existing asm use lower camel or mixed callback style such as cbForStateBusy, cbForStateGettingError, stateReadingFST, stateBusy, and stateCheckID3.
DVDFS storage naming distinguished BootInfo, FstStart, FstStringStart, MaxEntryNum, currentDirectory, and __DVDLongFileNameFlag.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the initial DVDInit guard from an sbss lbl load/branch into `if (!DVDInitialized)`.
Used `DVDInitialized = TRUE` after OSInitAlarm to match the original one-time initialization path.
Set `bootInfo = (void*) 0x80000000` and `IDShouldBe = 0x80000000` to match the original stores of a 0x80000000 base pointer.
Called `__OSSetInterruptHandler(0x15, __DVDInterruptHandler)` and `__OSUnmaskInterrupts(0x400)` with literal constants matching the removed assembly.
Used boot magic comparisons `0xE5207C22` and `0xD15EA5E` to reproduce the original JTAG, bootrom, and first-time bootrom reporting branches.
Kept unresolved or not-yet-recovered routines as extern calls rather than forcing broader decompilation.
Retained the remaining DVD assembly after DVDInit, minimizing risk to the large DVD state machine and command handling code.
Renamed sda21/sbss labels in assembly to match the new C extern names, ensuring C/asm symbol linkage stays consistent.

review_feedback.note: The PR dump contains no issue comments or review comments, so there is no detailed reviewer rationale beyond the approval.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that expand src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c beyond DVDInit.
Search for later recovery of the full boot info struct used by bootInfo->boot_magic.
Search for PRs that replace the fixed-address IO placeholder with named DVD hardware register definitions.
Find subsequent DVD PRs that decompile stateBusy, cbForStateBusy, DVDGetDriveStatus, DVDCheckDisk, or DVDCancelAsync.
Search for further renames of BootInfo versus bootInfo to check whether naming was normalized.
